channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: Donald Trump's doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As tariffs put trade between China and the US in peril, Chinese businesses ponder the future
item.description: The 145% tariff from the U.S. and the retaliatory 125% tariff from China are putting businesses doing trade between the U.S. and China on edge.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House authorizes military control of land along the border
item.description: In a historic move, the Department of Defense now has control of the Roosevelt Reservation, a 60-foot-wide federal strip.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ordered to unfreeze funding in dispute with Maine over transgender students
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze funds intended for a Maine child nutrition program that were suspended amid a disagreement between the state and the president over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court gives Trump wiggle room with mistaken deportation decision
item.description: A Supreme Court decision pushing for the return of a man mistakenly deported to a Salvadoran prison has both his lawyers and the Trump administration claiming victory, leaving unclear if and when Kilmar Abrego Garcia will return home.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: China put steep tariffs on U.S. exports. Farmers are worried
item.description: The first Trump administration spent $28 billion bailing out farmers during a trade war with China. The White House has said it's starting to look at how to help this time around.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: GOP frustrations with conservatives rise after budget fight
item.description: Frustrations are bubbling up among House Republicans after the conference — by the skin of its teeth — overcame internal disputes to adopt a framework for President Trump’s legislative agenda, a troubling sign for the group as it heads into the next, more difficult, step in achieving the president’s
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced deals Friday with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of punishing executive orders and require them to together provide hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of free legal services for causes his administration says it suppor
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington, DC, faces budget crisis after House leaves $1.1 billion hole in city coffers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the House of Representatives adjourned for a two-week recess Thursday, it left Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump opens window for a deal with Iran but issues warning if things don't work out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is betting that a beleaguered Iran is so vulnerable following a tumultuous 18 months in the Middle East that it might finally be ready to abandon its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge sides with Trump in allowing immigration enforcement in houses of worship
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday sided with the Trump administration in allowing immigration agents to conduct enforcement operations at houses of worship for now, despite a lawsuit filed by religious groups over the new policy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Now that they've passed a budget plan, the hard part begins for Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans narrowly got their budget plan over the finish line. Now comes the hard part. The resolution adopted this week was only a first step that allows Republicans to draft legislation that they can push through Congress without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beijing slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods in latest U.S.-China trade escalation
item.description: China signals the latest tariff hike will be its last round of tit-for-tat measures, prompting sharp falls in European shares, as Asian stocks end the day mixed.
item.dc:creator: Sherisse Pham, MPR News

item.title: Bessent moves to center of Trump World
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has stepped up his role on President Trump’s economic team, taking center stage on the president’s plans for tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Podcasts increasingly relevant to politicians, Adam Carolla says
item.description: In 2024, Republican Donald Trump guested on “The Joe Rogan Experience,” while Democrat Kamala Harris appeared on “Call Her Daddy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court says Trump administration must work to bring back mistakenly deported Maryland man
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration must work to bring back a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to prison in El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge allows requirement that everyone in the US illegally must register to move forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday allowed the Trump administration to move forward with a requirement that everyone in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Legislature moving bills as Friday deadline nears; ghost kitchens, open enrollment among winners
item.description: Up against a Friday deadline for bills to be passed out of committee, Nevada lawmakers are working this week to move mountains of legislation in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines flight carrying US lawmakers struck at DCA
item.description: No injuries were reported and the plane was on the ground at the time of impact, according to the agency.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Ilhan Omar will run for reelection in the U.S. House, not an open Minnesota Senate seat
item.description: U.S. Rep. Ilhan Omar had been considering a run for the seat currently held by Sen. Tina Smith. The DFLer will instead seek a fifth House term.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: U.S. says it is now monitoring immigrants' social media for antisemitism
item.description: Effective immediately, the government says it will begin screening immigrant social media for activity that officials think indicates support for antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
